# Forum Project — Personal Roadmap

Build order: server → static frontend → database → auth → features → polish.
Each phase ends with something you can actually run and test before moving on.

## Target file structure (grows phase by phase — see notes on each file)

```
forum/
├── go.mod
├── go.sum
├── Dockerfile
├── docker-compose.yml
├── README.md
├── cmd/
│   └── server/
│       └── main.go                # Phase 1, extended every phase
├── internal/
│   ├── router/
│   │   └── router.go              # Phase 1
│   ├── middleware/
│   │   └── middleware.go          # Phase 1 (logging), Phase 4 (auth guard), Phase 9 (errors)
│   ├── db/
│   │   ├── db.go                  # Phase 3
│   │   └── schema.sql             # Phase 3
│   ├── models/
│   │   ├── user.go                # Phase 3/4
│   │   ├── category.go            # Phase 3
│   │   ├── post.go                # Phase 5
│   │   ├── comment.go             # Phase 6
│   │   └── reaction.go            # Phase 7
│   ├── auth/
│   │   ├── password.go            # Phase 4
│   │   ├── session.go             # Phase 4
│   │   └── handlers.go            # Phase 4
│   └── handlers/
│       ├── posts.go               # Phase 5
│       ├── comments.go            # Phase 6
│       ├── reactions.go           # Phase 7
│       └── filters.go             # Phase 8
├── web/
│   ├── static/
│   │   ├── css/style.css          # Phase 2
│   │   └── js/
│   │       ├── main.js            # Phase 2 (nav/shared), Phase 5+ (fetch calls)
│   │       ├── auth.js            # Phase 4
│   │       ├── posts.js           # Phase 5
│   │       ├── comments.js        # Phase 6
│   │       └── reactions.js       # Phase 7
│   └── pages/
│       ├── index.html             # Phase 2
│       ├── login.html             # Phase 2
│       ├── register.html          # Phase 2
│       ├── new-post.html          # Phase 2
│       ├── post.html              # Phase 2
│       ├── category.html          # Phase 2
│       ├── profile.html           # Phase 2
│       └── 404.html               # Phase 1
└── (tests live alongside the code they test: internal/models/user_test.go, etc.)
```

---

## Phase 1 — Static HTTP Server

**Goal:** A Go server that serves static files and nothing else. No database, no
dynamic content. Prove the plumbing works before building on it.

**Build:**
1. `go mod init forum`, set up the folder tree above (empty placeholder files
   are fine for now).
2. `cmd/server/main.go`: start an `http.Server` on `:8080`.
3. `internal/router/router.go`: an `http.ServeMux` that:
   - Serves everything under `/static/` from `web/static/` via `http.FileServer`.
   - Serves `web/pages/index.html` at `/`.
   - Falls back to a custom `web/pages/404.html` (with a real `404` status
     code) for unmatched routes — don't let Go's default file-not-found page
     leak through.
4. `internal/middleware/middleware.go`: a logging middleware that wraps every
   request — log method, path, status code, and duration. Wrap the whole mux
   with it in `main.go`.
5. Graceful shutdown: listen for `SIGINT`/`SIGTERM`, call `server.Shutdown(ctx)`.
6. A one-line placeholder `index.html` and `404.html` so you have something
   to load (real design happens in Phase 2).

**Test before moving on:**
- `go run ./cmd/server`, then `curl -i http://localhost:8080/` → `200`, HTML body.
- `curl -i http://localhost:8080/nonsense` → `404`, your custom page, not Go's default.
- Add a placeholder `web/static/css/style.css`, confirm
  `curl -i http://localhost:8080/static/css/style.css` returns `200` with
  `Content-Type: text/css`.
- Check the log line appears for each request with correct status/duration.

---

## Phase 2 — Static Frontend Pages

**Goal:** Every page of the forum exists as real HTML/CSS, navigable by
clicking links, with no backend logic behind it yet. Use hardcoded/fake data
so the pages look real. This is where you decide what data each page will
eventually need — which directly informs the Phase 3 schema.

**Pages to build:**
- `index.html` — feed of posts (title, author, category tags, like/dislike
  counts, comment count) + nav bar (Login/Register if logged out, or
  username/logout if logged in — fake both states to check the look).
- `login.html`, `register.html` — forms, no submit logic yet.
- `new-post.html` — title, body, category checkboxes/multi-select.
- `post.html` — full post, comment list, comment form, like/dislike buttons.
- `category.html` — same as feed but scoped to one category (subforum view).
- `profile.html` — tabs or sections for "My posts" and "My liked posts".
- Shared `nav` and `footer` markup, shared `style.css`.

**Test before moving on:**
- Click through every page from the nav — no dead links.
- Resize the browser — nothing breaks at mobile width.
- Get a friend (or just yourself, fresh eyes) to look at it without
  explanation — can they tell what each button does?

**Why this before the database:** once these pages exist, the exact fields
each entity needs (does a post need a summary field? do comments need
threading?) are obvious from the HTML instead of guessed at.

---

## Phase 3 — Database Schema & Connection Layer

**Goal:** Now that the UI is real, design the schema to match it exactly.

**Build:**
1. Sketch the ERD (users, sessions, categories, posts, post_categories,
   comments, reactions) based on what Phase 2's pages actually display.
2. `internal/db/schema.sql`: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` for each entity,
   foreign keys, a unique index so a user can't react twice to the same
   post/comment.
3. `internal/db/db.go`: open SQLite with `_foreign_keys=on`,
   `_journal_mode=WAL`, `_busy_timeout=5000`; run the schema on startup
   (idempotent, safe every time).
4. `internal/models/user.go` + `category.go`: minimal repository methods —
   at least one `INSERT`, one `SELECT`. Seed the categories your Phase 2
   pages reference.

**Test before moving on:**
- Run the server, inspect the `.db` file with the `sqlite3` CLI — all tables
  exist, categories seeded.
- Write a temporary throwaway route (delete it after) that inserts and
  selects a user, confirm both work and duplicates are rejected.

---

## Phase 4 — Authentication

**Goal:** Real registration and login, wired to the Phase 2 forms.

**Build:**
1. `internal/auth/password.go`: bcrypt hash/compare wrappers.
2. `internal/models/user.go`: extend with whatever queries auth needs.
3. `internal/auth/session.go`: UUID session IDs, `sessions` table insert,
   expiry (pick a duration — e.g. 24h), cookie set/clear helpers.
4. `internal/auth/handlers.go`: `POST /api/register`, `POST /api/login`,
   `POST /api/logout`. Register checks for duplicate email (`409`), login
   checks password with bcrypt (`401` on mismatch), both set the session
   cookie on success.
5. `internal/middleware/middleware.go`: an auth-guard middleware that
   reads the cookie, validates the session isn't expired, and attaches the
   user to the request context (or rejects with `401`).
6. Wire `login.html`/`register.html` forms to these endpoints via
   `web/static/js/auth.js` (fetch + display errors inline, no `alert()`).
7. Nav bar in `main.js` reflects real logged-in/out state.

**Test before moving on:**
- Register a new user → succeeds, cookie set. Register same email again → `409`.
- Login with wrong password → `401`. Login correctly → cookie set, nav updates.
- Manually expire/delete a session row, confirm the guarded route rejects it.
- Logout clears the cookie and the nav reverts.

---

## Phase 5 — Posts & Categories

**Goal:** Registered users can create posts with categories; everyone can
read the feed.

**Build:**
1. `internal/models/post.go`: create post (+ insert into `post_categories`),
   list posts (feed), get single post by ID — with category names joined in.
2. `internal/handlers/posts.go`: `POST /api/posts` (auth required),
   `GET /api/posts` (public), `GET /api/posts/{id}` (public).
3. Wire `new-post.html` to submit via `posts.js`; wire `index.html` and
   `post.html` to fetch and render real data instead of hardcoded HTML.

**Test before moving on:**
- Create a post as a logged-in user with 2 categories → appears in feed
  with both category tags.
- Try creating a post while logged out → `401`, form shows the error.
- View a single post directly by URL/ID → correct content.

---

## Phase 6 — Comments

**Build:**
1. `internal/models/comment.go`: create, list-by-post.
2. `internal/handlers/comments.go`: `POST /api/posts/{id}/comments` (auth
   required), `GET /api/posts/{id}/comments` (public).
3. Wire `post.html` comment section to `comments.js`.

**Test before moving on:**
- Add a comment as a logged-in user → appears immediately.
- Guest sees existing comments, no comment form (or a disabled one with a
  "log in to comment" prompt).
- Comment count on `index.html` feed matches the post's actual comment count.

---

## Phase 7 — Likes & Dislikes

**Build:**
1. `internal/models/reaction.go`: upsert-style toggle (like → dislike →
   remove), enforced by the unique index from Phase 3.
2. `internal/handlers/reactions.go`: `POST /api/posts/{id}/react`,
   `POST /api/comments/{id}/react` (both auth required; body `{type: "like"|"dislike"}`).
3. Wire like/dislike buttons in `reactions.js` — counts update without a
   full page reload.

**Test before moving on:**
- Like a post → count goes up, button shows active state.
- Click dislike while like is active → like count drops, dislike count
  rises (not both counted).
- Click the same button again → reaction removed, count reverts.
- Guest clicking like/dislike → `401`, prompted to log in.
- Refresh the page → reaction state and counts persist correctly.

---

## Phase 8 — Filters

**Build:**
1. `internal/handlers/filters.go` (or extend `posts.go`):
   `GET /api/posts?category={id}` (public), `GET /api/posts?mine=true`
   (auth required — posts by logged-in user), `GET /api/posts?liked=true`
   (auth required — posts the logged-in user has liked).
2. Wire `category.html` to the category filter; wire `profile.html` tabs to
   the "mine" and "liked" filters.

**Test before moving on:**
- Category filter returns only posts in that category.
- "My posts" as guest → `401` or redirect to login, not a silent empty list.
- "My posts"/"My liked posts" as a logged-in user → correct, user-scoped
  results only (verify with two different test accounts).

---

## Phase 9 — Error Handling, Tests, Docker, Deploy

**Build:**
1. Consistent HTTP status codes across every handler (400 bad input, 401
   unauthenticated, 403 forbidden, 404 not found, 405 method not allowed,
   500 unexpected) — audit every handler written so far against this list.
2. Custom error pages/JSON error bodies (be consistent — pick JSON errors
   for `/api/*`, HTML pages for direct navigation).
3. Unit tests: at minimum `internal/models/*_test.go` (repository logic)
   and `internal/auth/*_test.go` (password hashing, session expiry logic).
4. `Dockerfile` (multi-stage; cgo stays enabled for `go-sqlite3` — don't use
   a scratch/distroless final image, use `debian-slim` + `libsqlite3-0`).
5. `docker-compose.yml` with a named volume for the SQLite file.
6. Final README: setup, run, env vars, architecture summary.

**Test before calling it done:**
- `go test ./...` passes.
- `docker build` succeeds, `docker run` serves the full app, data survives
  a container restart (volume works).
- Manually hit every error case (double-submit register, bad login,
  unauthenticated post creation, nonexistent post ID, wrong HTTP method on
  an endpoint) and confirm the status code + message are sane.
